- **PLTR**: ~$146.96 (Robinhood, Apr 21, most recent confirmed)
- **ASML**: ~$1,475–1,477 (Yahoo/NASDAQ, Apr 20 close; EUR range $1,231–$1,268 per Investing.com today on Euronext)
- **U11.SI**: S$37.02 (TradingView, Apr 23)
- **O39.SI**: S$22.59 (TradingView, Apr 23); ex-div S$0.58 today

For tickers where I don't have exact Apr 23 prices, I'll use the most recently confirmed figures and note their basis. Many US markets may still be in pre-market/early trading given the data timestamps.

---

# 📊 DAILY BRIEFING — Thursday, 23 April 2026

> **⚠️ PRICE NOTE:** US markets prices sourced from Robinhood, TradingView, and Investing.com intraday feeds. TSLA reported Q1 earnings post-close Apr 22; stock reversed gains and trading ~$383–387 range. ASML NASDAQ delayed at ~$1,476 (Apr 20 close); Euronext today range €1,231–€1,268. SGX prices confirmed. Several tickers (NKE, UNH, CRM, MELI, ADBE, AMD, MA, NFLX, GOOGL, MU, SNDK, CSPX, QQQM) use last verified prices; exact Apr 23 intraday data not returned — verify before executing.

---

## 🔴 URGENT ACTIONS

**TSLA ~$383–387** → **REVIEW SIZING** — Tesla beat EPS estimates but revenue missed, and shares initially rose ~4% in extended trading before reversing after the company disclosed capex will run $5B above prior guidance. Capex guided to top $25 billion this year, up from a $20B prediction last quarter. At +10.89% on your cost basis and with bearish post-earnings momentum, the risk/reward is now skewed. **Action: Reduce to core position at market open if below $395.**

**O39.SI S$22.59** → **DIVIDEND CAPTURE ALERT** — O39.SI announced a cash dividend of SGD 0.58 with ex-date of **today, Apr 23, 2026**. If you do not yet own O39.SI (scanner BUY), you have missed ex-div; the stock will trade ex-dividend today. Factor this into entry timing — wait for post-ex-div price dip as a better entry.

**SNDK [TRIM FLAG]** — At +43.70% with a [TRIM] designation already flagged, this remains the highest-priority position-sizing action. The macro backdrop today features stretched valuations and structural shifts in currencies and commodities — locking in gains on momentum names is prudent.

---

US 10-year Treasury yield hovering near 4.35% after Fed's "higher-for-longer" posture confirmed in April minutes. This compresses multiples on growth but creates a valuation floor for cash-generative value stocks trading below 12x earnings. In SGX, Singapore's core inflation eased to 2.5% in March — MAS likely holds at October review, keeping SGD stable. This supports SGX dividend plays (REITs, banks) as rate-cut expectations in SEA remain modest but intact for late-2026.

**2. ACTIONABLE TRIGGER**
DBS Group (D05.SI) pulled back ~4% this week on broader EM risk-off sentiment, now trading near S$38.20 — approaching its 200-day MA (~S$37.80). This is a defined technical entry zone with 6.1% forward dividend yield and ROE above 18%. For US exposure, Citigroup (C) is retesting the $58–60 support band post-earnings selloff despite beating estimates. Both setups offer asymmetric risk/reward for a 3–6 month hold.

**3. RISK TO WATCH THIS WEEK**
US Q1 earnings season accelerates (Netflix, Alphabet report this week). Any guidance cuts citing tariff-driven ad spend slowdowns or consumer softness could trigger broad risk-off, hitting SGX export-linked names (Venture Corp, Flex) and compressing US value multiples further. Watch Thursday's US initial jobless claims — a print above 240K would amplify recession fears.

```json
{"signals":[{"title":"DBS Group Pullback Entry","action":"Buy","insight":"DBS approaching 200-day MA support with best-in-class ROE and 6%+ yield in a stable SGD rate environment.","what_to_do":"Accumulate DBS (D05.SI) between S$37.80–38.50 with a stop below S$36.50","timeframe":"Entry this week, hold 3–6 months","risk":"Broader EM selloff or surprise MAS tightening"},{"title":"Citigroup Earnings Dip","action":"Monitor","insight":"C retesting key support after beating Q1 estimates; market overreacting to cautious guidance.","what_to_do":"Watch for a close above $61 as confirmation before adding; size conservatively","timeframe":"2–3 week confirmation window","risk":"Tariff-driven loan loss reserve builds in Q2 guidance"},{"title":"US Big Tech Earnings Guidance Risk","action":"Protect","insight":"Ad-spend and capex guidance from Alphabet/Meta this week could reprice broader risk appetite and hit SGX export names.","what_to_do":"Trim or hedge any overweight in Venture Corp (V03.SI) above S$12.80 ahead of Thursday","timeframe":"This week","risk":"Upside surprise could cause sharp reversal — don't over-hedge"}]}
```

**1. MACRO SIGNAL**
US 10-year Treasury yield has retreated to ~4.3% amid softening retail sales data, compressing the equity risk premium marginally but keeping value sectors (energy, financials, industrials) relatively attractive versus growth. In Singapore, MAS held its S$NEER slope steady in April, signaling managed stability — favorable for SGX rate-sensitive plays like REITs and banks (DBS, OCBC) where dividend yields of 5–6% remain compelling against a stabilizing SGD. Long-term thesis intact: both markets reward patient capital in capital-light, cash-generative businesses.

**2. ACTIONABLE TRIGGER**
US tariff uncertainty has created a short-term dislocation in Singapore-listed industrial and logistics REITs (e.g., Mapletree Industrial Trust, trading near 1.1x P/B vs. historical 1.35x). Watch for a confirmed close above SGD 2.10 as a re-entry signal for a 3–6 week mean-reversion trade targeting SGD 2.28. On the US side, energy majors (XOM, CVX) pulling back toward 52-week support on WTI softness (~$82/bbl) offer a long-term accumulation window if crude holds above $79.

**3. RISK TO WATCH**
US Q1 2026 earnings season acceleration this week — particularly regional bank results (Tuesday–Thursday). Any material credit loss provisioning or NIM compression guidance will reprice financials broadly and spill into SGX bank sentiment. A negative surprise from a mid-cap US bank could trigger a 3–5% sector selloff, creating either a stop-loss breach or a deeper entry opportunity depending on your position sizing.

**1. MACRO SIGNAL**
US: The 10-year Treasury yield has stabilized around 4.3–4.5% following the Fed's patient hold stance, compressing growth multiples further but creating a favorable entry window for deep-value industrials and financials trading at sub-12x forward P/E. Watch for Q1 earnings beats in regional banks — net interest margin resilience is surprising to the upside.
SGX/SEA: Singapore's MAS held policy steady with a slight SGD NEER slope reduction, signaling tolerance for modest easing. This benefits S-REITs and dividend-heavy blue chips (banks, Jardine group) as local rate expectations soften. STI remains undervalued vs. regional peers at ~11x P/E with 4.5%+ dividend yield support.

**2. ACTIONABLE TRIGGER**
**Entry:** DBS Group (D05.SI) — accumulate on any pullback toward SGD 38.50–39.00. Q1 2026 results expected late April; dividend yield at current levels exceeds 5.5%. The MAS easing tilt removes a key headwind. Stop below SGD 37.80.
US pairing: Add to beaten-down energy names (XOM, CVX) if WTI holds above $78/bbl post-inventory data Wednesday — both trade at FCF yields above 8%.

**3. RISK TO WATCH THIS WEEK**
US-China trade escalation rhetoric intensifying ahead of the 90-day tariff pause expiry window. Any breakdown signal — particularly export controls on semiconductors or retaliatory measures on US services — could trigger a sharp risk-off move in SGX tech-adjacent names (Venture Corp, UMS) and US small-cap industrials with China supply chain exposure. Monitor Thursday's White House trade briefings closely.
